title: malcolmia patula var. canescens
intro: Malcolmia patula var. canescens, also known as hairy whitlow grass, is a plant species that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also known for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, Malcolmia patula var. canescens can be used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil to harness its medicinal properties.
